parsed.simulated_user_message: Every time I finish a report, I have to rush to the hospital, and the next thing I know I'm up all night. I keep thinking my body is going to give out, and I can't focus on what I need to do for my future at the firm. It's like I'm stuck on a treadmill with no end.

parsed.reasoning: I'm a supportive companion to this person, and they need me to reflect their emotions right now. Given their express_emotion intent, I'll use complex_reflection and support strategies to acknowledge their feelings. The evidence shows they're overwhelmed by the cycle of work and hospital visits, fearing their health and job are at risk. My entry will acknowledge the treadmill feeling they described, showing I understand the sense of being stuck.
parsed.final_response: You're on this treadmill, feeling like you're running non-stop, and it's exhausting. Every report finished means rushing to the hospital, and then you're up all night, worrying your body will give out. It's like you're losing control, and the future at the firm is getting blurry. Yeah, it sounds like you're really struggling to catch your breath in all this.
